This shows that evolution has a bad inner alignment problem.
The main claim is: Thus, the policies trained on this have all the nice properties of linear RL algorithms and are, in fact, highly controllable.

If you have two policies, then you can construct a linear combination of them, and this is the optimal policy for a reward function which is a linear combination of the reward functions used to train each policy individually.

The main claim is: Rae et al) (summarized by Rohin): This paper details the training of the Gopher family of large language models (LLMs), the biggest of which is named Gopher and has 280 billion parameters.
With 10x more computation, it would suggest training a 5.5x larger model with 1.8x as much data.
